metadata.name: Information Requirements Hierarchy
metadata.description: Hierarchy of information requirements defined in ISO 19650 showing how organizational objectives translate through OIR, AIR, PIR, and EIR to drive information production and exchange.
metadata.author: ISO19650 Knowledge Maps

nodes.label: Organization
nodes.description: Organization responsible for defining objectives and information requirements, typically the appointing party.

nodes.label: Organizational Objectives
nodes.description: Strategic, tactical and operational objectives of the organization that drive the need for information.

nodes.label: Organizational Information Requirements (OIR)
nodes.description: Information required to support organizational objectives, including strategic business operation, asset management, portfolio planning, regulatory duties, and policy-making.

nodes.label: Asset Information Requirements (AIR)
nodes.description: Information required to support asset management decisions during the operational phase, derived from OIR and specified for each trigger event.

nodes.label: Project Information Requirements (PIR)
nodes.description: Information required to support project delivery decisions at key decision points, derived from OIR and/or AIR.

nodes.label: Exchange Information Requirements (EIR)
nodes.description: Information requirements defined for appointments, specifying what, when, and how information is to be delivered at information exchange milestones.

nodes.label: Information Exchange
nodes.description: Act of satisfying an information requirement through delivery of information containers at defined milestones.

nodes.label: Information Production
nodes.description: Activity of producing project or asset information to meet exchange information requirements.

nodes.label: Level of Information Need
nodes.description: Framework defining the extent and granularity of information required to satisfy each information requirement, preventing delivery of too much information.

nodes.label: Information Container
nodes.description: Named persistent set of information retrievable from within a file, system or application storage hierarchy.

nodes.label: Information Model
nodes.description: Set of structured and unstructured information containers forming the project or asset information model.

nodes.label: Project Information Model (PIM)
nodes.description: Information model used during project delivery, specified by EIR.

nodes.label: Asset Information Model (AIM)
nodes.description: Information model used during asset operation, specified by AIR.

nodes.label: Trigger Event
nodes.description: Planned or unplanned event that changes an asset or its status during its lifecycle, resulting in information exchange.

nodes.label: Key Decision Point
nodes.description: Point in time during the project when a decision crucial to the direction or viability of the asset is made.

nodes.label: Appointing Party
nodes.description: The party defining information requirements and accepting information exchanges.

evidence.quote: The appointing party specifically links asset management to the achievement of its business objectives through asset management policies, strategies and plans.
Organizational information requirements explain the information needed to answer or inform high-level strategic objectives within the appointing party.
Asset information requirements derive from organizational information requirements.
Project information requirements derive from organizational information requirements.
Asset information requirements inform project information requirements when projects are undertaken to deliver or modify assets.
Exchange information requirements specify those detailed pieces of information needed to answer the project information requirements.
AIR received by a lead appointed party can be sub-divided and passed on in any of its own appointments, augmented with its own information requirements.
A set of AIR should be prepared in response to each trigger event during asset operation.
A set of information requirements should be prepared for each of the appointing party's key decision points during the project.
The level of information need should be determined by the minimum amount of information needed to answer each relevant requirement.
The level of information need should be determined by the minimum amount of information needed to answer each relevant requirement.
Information requirements guide the planning of information delivery.
Information containers are produced by delivery teams.
An information model is a set of structured and unstructured information containers.
Project information model relates to the delivery phase.
Asset information model relates to the operational phase.
Information developed during project delivery contributes to the asset information model.
Exchange information requirements specify the information to be delivered at information exchanges.
Information exchange is the act of satisfying an information requirement.
The appointing party establishes its organizational information requirements.
The appointing party establishes their exchange information requirements.
